children.heading: CIFIA program
children.label: 1 CIFIA program
children.text: The term “CIFIA program” means the carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure finance and innovation program established under .section 16372(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: publishes a publicly available tariff containing the just and reasonable rates, terms, and conditions of nondiscriminatory service; and

children.label: B
children.text: holds itself out to provide transportation services to the public for a fee.

children.label: A
children.text: contingent on those funds being made available in law at a future date; and

children.label: B
children.text: not an obligation of the Federal Government.

children.label: i
children.text: development-phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, permitting, preliminary engineering and design work, and other preconstruction activities;

children.label: ii
children.text: construction, reconstruction, rehabilitation, replacement, and acquisition of real property (including land relating to the project and improvements to land), environmental mitigation, construction contingencies, and acquisition and installation of equipment (including labor); and

children.label: iii
children.text: capitalized interest necessary to meet market requirements, reasonably required reserve funds, capital issuance expenses, and other carrying costs during construction; and

children.label: i
children.text: the cost of legal counsel and technical consultants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any subsidy amount paid in accordance with or .section 16372(c)(3)(B)(ii) of this titlesection 16373(b)(6)(B)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Federal credit instrument
children.label: 5 Federal credit instrument
children.text: The term “Federal credit instrument” means a secured loan or loan guarantee authorized to be provided under the CIFIA program with respect to a project.

children.heading: Lender
children.label: 6 Lender
children.text: The term “lender” means a qualified institutional buyer (as defined in section 230.144A(a) of title 17, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation), commonly known as Rule 144A(a) of the Securities and Exchange Commission and issued under the Securities Act of 1933 ( et seq.)), that is not a Federal qualified institutional buyer.15 U.S.C. 77a

children.label: A
children.text: describes the project and the location, purpose, and cost of the project;

children.label: B
children.text: outlines the proposed financial plan, including the requested credit and grant assistance and the proposed obligor;

children.label: C
children.text: provides a status of environmental review; and

children.label: D
children.text: provides information regarding satisfaction of other eligibility requirements of the CIFIA program.

children.heading: Loan guarantee
children.label: 8 Loan guarantee
children.text: The term “loan guarantee” means any guarantee or other pledge by the Secretary to pay all or part of the principal of, and interest on, a loan made to an obligor, or debt obligation issued by an obligor, in each case funded by a lender.

children.label: i
children.text: a project of high priority under ; orsection 16372(c)(3)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a project covered under ;section 16372(c)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: does not provide for a current obligation of Federal funds; and

children.label: I
children.text: the availability of future funds being made available to carry out the CIFIA program; and

children.label: II
children.text: the satisfaction of all conditions for the provision of credit assistance under the CIFIA program, including ;section 16373(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: establish the maximum amounts and general terms and conditions of the Federal credit instruments or grants;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify the 1 or more revenue sources that will secure the repayment of the Federal credit instruments;

children.label: I
children.text: compliance with all applicable requirements specified under the CIFIA program, including sections 16372(d) and 16373(b)(1) of this title; and

children.label: II
children.text: the availability of funds to carry out the CIFIA program; and

children.label: v
children.text: require that contingent commitments shall result in a financial close and obligation of credit or grant assistance by not later than 4 years after the date of entry into the agreement or release of the commitment, as applicable, unless otherwise extended by the Secretary.

children.heading: Obligor
children.label: 10 Obligor
children.text: The term “obligor” means a corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, non-Federal governmental entity, agency, or instrumentality, or other entity that is liable for payment of the principal of, or interest on, a Federal credit instrument.

children.heading: Produced in the United States
children.label: 11 Produced in the United States
children.text: The term “produced in the United States”, with respect to iron and steel, means that all manufacturing processes for the iron and steel, including the application of any coating, occurs within the United States.

children.heading: Project
children.label: 12 Project
children.text: The term “project” means a project for common carrier carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure or associated equipment, including pipeline, shipping, rail, or other transportation infrastructure and associated equipment, that will transport or handle carbon dioxide captured from anthropogenic sources or ambient air, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Project obligation
children.label: 13 Project obligation
children.text: The term “project obligation” means any note, bond, debenture, or other debt obligation issued by an obligor in connection with the financing of a project, other than a Federal credit instrument.

children.heading: Secured loan
children.label: 14 Secured loan
children.text: The term “secured loan” means a direct loan to an obligor or a debt obligation issued by an obligor and purchased by the Secretary, in each case funded by the Secretary in connection with the financing of a project under .section 16373 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: calculated on a net present value basis; and

children.label: B
children.text: excluding administrative costs and any incidental effects on governmental receipts or outlays in accordance with the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990 ( et seq.).2 U.S.C. 661

children.label: A
children.text: on which the project commences transportation of carbon dioxide; or

children.label: B
children.text: of a comparable event to the event described in subparagraph (A), as determined by the Secretary and specified in the project credit agreement.
